title: First Linux Login
description: Connect to an isolated training server, locate hidden training material, and complete the exercise from inside the lab environment.

workstation.reason: This lab works best from a Linux terminal. Windows Desktop is supported if you prefer a full desktop — use OpenSSH or PowerShell to SSH to the training server.

tasks: Open the lab terminal and connect to the training server
Search the environment for hidden training material (try ls -la and find)
Read the file and note its contents
Submit your answers in the lab panel — the lab completes automatically when all objectives are done

objectivesPublic.label: Connect to the training server
objectivesPublic.text: SSH from the Lab Terminal using your session credentials.
objectivesPublic.hint: Session credentials are unique to this run — copy them from the lab panel into your SSH command.

objectivesPublic.label: Locate the hidden training file
objectivesPublic.text: Search for dotfiles — the real flag may be outside your home directory.
objectivesPublic.prompt: What is the hidden training filename?
objectivesPublic.hint: Hidden names start with a dot. Search common training paths; ignore obvious decoys in your home folder.

objectivesPublic.label: Read and identify the file contents
objectivesPublic.text: Open the hidden file you identified and note what it contains.
objectivesPublic.prompt: What text is inside the hidden training file?
objectivesPublic.hint: Print the file to the terminal — compare with decoy files so you submit the real training content.
